assertions.text: V1 was traveling northbound on a two-lane, two-way roadway negotiating a left hand curve.
V1 departed the roadway on the right side.
The front plane of V1 contacted a utility pole.
V1 departed the roadway and then the front plane contacted a utility pole (departure occurred before the pole impact).
There was a single impact where V1's front plane contacted a utility pole.
V1 came to final rest in the middle of the roadway partially in the northbound and southbound lanes.
When at final rest, V1 was facing southeast.
V1's front plane contact with the utility pole constitutes a direct frontal impact.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows V1 speed around [83.0 km/h at t=-4.7 s](T2) and [80.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2) and steering peak of [27.0 deg at t=-0.2 s](T2), but vehicle heading (northbound) and road curvature (left-hand) are not recorded so direction and curve negotiation are unverifiable.

No GPS lateral position or lane data are available in the telemetry; only speed ([83.0 km/h first](T2), [80.0 km/h last](T2)) and crash pulse are present. Departure from roadway (right-side) cannot be determined from these channels.

EDR crash-pulse shows a large longitudinal cumulative delta-V with final value [ -75.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T2) and the CISS detected a dominant longitudinal impact delta-V of [ -75.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T3), consistent with a frontal (front-plane) impact to a rigid object like a pole.

Telemetry lacks GPS position or lane-displacement timestamps; speed series ([83.0 km/h to 80.0 km/h between t=-4.7 and t=0.0](T2)) and delta-V timing ([impact peak at t=0.24 s](T2)) do not indicate where roadway departure occurred relative to impact.

The EDR summary reports a single dominant crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V peaking at [ -76.153 km/h at ~274.5 ms in the EDR summary](T1) and compute_stats shows final longitudinal delta-V [ -75.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T2); detect_events reports one significant delta-V event on delta_v_long (magnitude [75.6 km/h](T3)). No additional distinct impact spikes are detected in the crash-pulse channels.

Gyro or orientation channels needed to determine rotation direction are not available or axis alignment is undocumented; detect_events shows an accel spike on accel_pedal ([23.5 g peak at t=-0.7 s](T3)) but that does not specify clockwise rotation. Therefore post-impact clockwise rotation is unverifiable.

Final rest location relative to roadway lanes is not recorded; only speed ([80.0 km/h at t=0.0](T2)) and crash-pulse data are present. Position-in-roadway (middle, straddling lanes) cannot be determined from sensors.

Vehicle heading at final rest is not provided in the telemetry. IMU axes are device-frame and heading inference needs GPS heading or clear rotation integration, which are not available, so facing southeast is unverifiable.

The large negative longitudinal cumulative delta-V (final [ -75.6 km/h](T2) with EDR summary max [ -76.153 km/h](T1)) indicates a severe deceleration into an object ahead; this pattern is consistent with a direct frontal impact of the front plane into a rigid obstacle.

result.rationale: Telemetry strongly supports a single, severe longitudinal frontal impact (large negative delta-V of about [ -75.6 km/h](T2) / [ -76.153 km/h](T1)), but lacks GPS/position/heading data needed to verify roadway side departure, lane position, rotation direction, and final facing.

citations.claim: 83.0 km/h to 80.0 km/h between t=-4.7 and t=0.0
-76.153 km/h at ~274.5 ms in the EDR summary

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
